criteria.label: State the convexity definition and explain the local-to-global guarantee.
criteria.example: Write the chord inequality for f; then explain in one sentence why gradient descent on convex logistic regression cannot settle in a suboptimal local minimum.

criteria.label: Classify critical points using Hessian eigenvalues.
criteria.example: Given Hessian eigenvalues [2, 5] at a critical point, label it a local minimum; given [-1, 3], label it a saddle.

criteria.label: Distinguish convex from non-convex examples and justify with a second-order test.
criteria.example: Show f(x)=x^2 is convex (f''(x)=2 >= 0) while g(x)=x^4 - 4x^2 has g''(0) < 0, hence g is non-convex.

criteria.label: Relate non-convexity to neural training practice (seeds, schedules, saddles).
criteria.example: Explain why you would run three random seeds on an MLP but not on logistic regression, citing local minima vs global optimum guarantees.
